en: Does your company operate as a Commercial Motor Carrier in Canada as regulated by federal or provincial regulations?
Does your company have a written program that addresses all NSC and/or Provincial requirements\r\nfor commercial carriers?
Does this program apply to and include all company drivers and current subcontractors or\r\nleased drivers?

Does this program include policies and procedures relating to Dangerous Goods hazard management\r\npractices (bill of lading and placard descriptions, placard site and in-transit management practices,\r\nproduct segregation and compatibility practices,other)?
Does your company keep a complete record of driver files that comply with Transport Canada requirements for all company and sub/leased drivers for current year and 4 years prior?

Does your company keep a record of all company and sub-leased driver convictions, collisions reportable to local Police or RCMP, and any administrative penalties imposed under safety laws for current year and 4 years prior?
Are driver logbooks retained for at least 6 months?
Do driver logbooks comply with Transport Canada requirements?
Does each vehicle file contain all maintenance and inspections records for current and 4 prior years?
Does your company use light duty vehicles in the course of completing job duties?
Are all company light vehicles outfitted with airbags for both the driver and front seat passenger (i.e.dual airbags); first registered after 1 July 2006?
Is the fire extinguisher securely mounted on a bracket and located so that it is easily accessible in\r\nan emergency without becoming a hazard in case of an accident?
Are all light vehicles equipped with a securely stowed first aid kit?
Does your company use heavy duty vehicles in the course of completing job duties?
Are all heavy goods vehicles fitted with anti-lock braking systems (ABS)?
Are all heavy goods vehicles fitted with a securely stowed first aid kit and flashlight?
Are the emergency exit door(s) clearly marked with a sign (this includes buses with only a single\r\nentry/exit door)?
Does your company carry cargo in the course of completing job duties?

Are all drivers trained to meet the cargo securement requirements of best practices, the
National Safety Code Standard #10 and industry best practices (as recommended by the Petroleum
Services Association of Canada (PSAC) and the Canadian Association of Oilwell Drilling
Contractors (CAODC))?
Does your company employ safe journey management (SJM) procedures in the course of\r\ncompleting job duties?
What companies does your company use for Logistic and Freight Forwarding Services?
